debt_collection.name: Debt Collection Notice
debt_collection.governing_law: Australian Consumer Law (ACL), ASIC Act 2001, National Credit Code
key_rights: Right to request written verification of the debt
Debt collectors cannot contact you at unreasonable times or with unreasonable frequency
Limitation period: typically 6 years from when debt became due
Right to complain to Australian Financial Complaints Authority (AFCA) — free
National Debt Helpline: 1800 007 007 (free financial counselling)
Collectors cannot make false or misleading representations about the debt
common_violations: Threatening legal action the collector cannot or does not intend to take
Contacting third parties about the debt without authority
Attempting to collect a statute-barred debt without disclosing this
Using coercive, harassing, or unconscionable conduct (breach of ACL)
Not identifying themselves as a debt collector
debt_collection.forum: AFCA (Australian Financial Complaints Authority) or Local Court
debt_collection.template_response: I write in response to your notice dated [DATE]. I request written verification of this debt, including the name of the original creditor, the amount alleged to be owed, and proof of your authority to collect this debt. I am aware of the Australian Consumer Law and ASIC debt collection guidelines. I reserve the right to lodge a complaint with AFCA if collection conduct is in breach of applicable guidelines.

eviction_notice.name: Eviction / Termination of Tenancy Notice
key_rights: Landlord must provide written notice with the reason for termination
Notice periods vary by state and reason: typically 14–90 days
Right to challenge termination at relevant tribunal (VCAT, NCAT, QCAT)
Cannot be forcibly removed without a tribunal order
Bond must be lodged with state bond authority and returned unless deductions justified
common_violations: Inadequate notice period for the reason given
Retaliatory eviction after tenant exercises their rights
Landlord entering without required notice (24-48 hours in most states)
Wrongful claim against bond without supporting evidence
eviction_notice.forum: State tenancy tribunal (VCAT/NCAT/QCAT depending on state)
eviction_notice.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of your notice dated [DATE]. I dispute the validity of this notice and intend to apply to the relevant tribunal for a hearing. I request that you refrain from any further action until the matter is resolved by the tribunal. I am aware that you cannot forcibly remove me without a tribunal order.

court_summons.name: Court Summons / Statement of Claim
court_summons.governing_law: Federal Court Rules, Uniform Civil Procedure Rules (state), Local Court Rules
key_rights: 28 days to file a Defence in most jurisdictions
Failure to respond results in default judgment
Small claims track (under $20,000 in most states) — simplified process
Right to free legal advice from Community Legal Centres
Right to apply to set aside default judgment if proper service not completed
common_violations: Improper service of documents
Claim filed in wrong jurisdiction
Claim is statute-barred
court_summons.forum: Local Court / Magistrates Court or Federal Court
court_summons.template_response: I acknowledge receipt of the Statement of Claim [Claim Number]. I intend to file my Defence within the required timeframe. I dispute the claim in its entirety and will provide detailed grounds in my Defence.

employment_termination.name: Termination / Redundancy Letter
employment_termination.governing_law: Fair Work Act 2009, National Employment Standards (NES)
key_rights: 21 days from dismissal to lodge an Unfair Dismissal application with Fair Work Commission
Redundancy pay based on years of service under NES
Minimum notice periods based on length of service (1–5 weeks)
Right to ask employer for reasons for dismissal in writing
Right to bring a support person to any dismissal meeting
General Protections claim if dismissed for exercising a workplace right
common_violations: Dismissal without valid reason and proper procedure (harsh, unjust, or unreasonable)
Failure to pay correct redundancy entitlements under NES
Insufficient notice or notice pay
Dismissal related to taking leave, union activity, or making a workplace complaint
employment_termination.template_response: I acknowledge my termination communicated on [DATE]. I request written reasons for my termination. I note that I have [X] years of service and am entitled to [notice period/redundancy pay] under the National Employment Standards and the Fair Work Act 2009. I reserve my rights to lodge a claim with the Fair Work Commission within 21 days.

consumer_notice.name: Consumer / Company Notice
consumer_notice.governing_law: Australian Consumer Law (ACL), Competition and Consumer Act 2010
key_rights: Consumer guarantees under ACL cannot be excluded by contract
Right to repair, replacement, or refund for major failures
Right to complain to ACCC or state consumer affairs agency
Small claims at state tribunal for disputes typically under $20,000–$100,000
Right to a refund if goods do not match description or are not of acceptable quality
common_violations: Attempting to exclude statutory consumer guarantees
Misleading or deceptive conduct in trade or commerce
Unconscionable conduct
Unfair contract terms in standard form consumer contracts
consumer_notice.forum: ACCC, State Consumer Affairs, or relevant Tribunal
consumer_notice.template_response: I write to formally notify you of a dispute under the Australian Consumer Law. I purchased [product/service] on [date] for [amount]. The [product/service] fails to meet the consumer guarantees under the ACL. I require [repair/replacement/refund] within 14 days. If unresolved, I will lodge a complaint with the ACCC and the relevant state consumer tribunal.
